revision.text:
'''新田原'''
* 新田原（しんでんはら） - [[福島県]][[南会津郡]][[南会津町]]の地名。 * 新田原（しんでんばる） - [[福岡県]][[行橋市]]大字道場寺付近の通称。 ** [[新田原駅]]（しんでんばるえき） - 福岡県行橋市大字道場寺にある[[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[日豊本線]]の駅。近隣に[[航空自衛隊]]の[[築城基地]]があり、しばしば後述の新田原基地と混同される。 * 新田原（しんたばる） - [[長崎県]][[諫早市]][[小長井町]]の地名。小長井町新田原。旧[[北高来郡]]小長井町新田原名。 * 新田原（にゅうたばる） - [[宮崎県]][[児湯郡]][[新富町]]大字新田にある地区の通称。旧宮崎県児湯郡[[新田村 (宮崎県)|新田村]]（にゅうたそん）字新田原。 ** [[新田原基地]]（にゅうたばるきち） - 宮崎県児湯郡新富町大字新田にある[[航空自衛隊]]の基地。
== 参考文献 == * [[角川日本地名大辞典]] 各巻
== 関連項目 == * {{Intitle}} * [[田原]]
{{Aimai}} [[Category:同名の地名]]

'''新田原''' ==一覧== * 新田原（しんでんはら） - [[福島県]][[南会津郡]][[南会津町]]の地名。 * 新田原（しんでんばる） - [[福岡県]][[行橋市]]大字道場寺付近の通称。 ** [[新田原駅]]（しんでんばるえき） - 福岡県行橋市大字道場寺にある[[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[日豊本線]]の駅。近隣に[[航空自衛隊]]の[[築城基地]]があり、しばしば後述の新田原基地と混同される。 * 新田原（しんたばる） - [[長崎県]][[諫早市]][[小長井町]]の地名。小長井町新田原。旧[[北高来郡]]小長井町新田原名。 * 新田原（にゅうたばる） - [[宮崎県]][[児湯郡]][[新富町]]大字新田にある地区の通称。旧宮崎県児湯郡[[新田村 (宮崎県)|新田村]]（にゅうたそん）字新田原。 ** [[新田原基地]]（にゅうたばるきち） - 宮崎県児湯郡新富町大字新田にある[[航空自衛隊]]の基地。
== 参考文献 == * [[角川日本地名大辞典]] 各巻
== 関連項目 == * {{Intitle}} * [[田原]]
{{Aimai}} [[Category:同名の地名]]

== 参考文献 == * [[角川日本地名大辞典]] 各巻
== 関連項目 == * {{Intitle}} * {{Prefix}} * [[田原 (曖昧さ回避)]] * [[原田 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Aimai}} [[Category:同名の地名]]

'''新田原''' ==一覧== * 新田原（しんでんはら） - [[福島県]][[南会津郡]][[南会津町]]の地名。 * 新田原（しんでんばる） - [[福岡県]][[行橋市]]大字道場寺付近の通称。 ** [[新田原駅]]（しんでんばるえき） - 福岡県行橋市大字道場寺にある[[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[日豊本線]]の駅。近隣に[[航空自衛隊]]の[[築城基地]]があり、しばしば後述の新田原基地と混同される。 * 新田原（しんたばる） - [[長崎県]][[諫早市]][[小長井町]]の地名。小長井町新田原。旧[[北高来郡]]小長井町新田原名。 * 新田原（にゅうたばる） - [[宮崎県]][[児湯郡]][[新富町]]大字新田にある地区の通称。旧宮崎県児湯郡[[新田村 (宮崎県)|新田村]]（にゅうたそん）字新田原。 ** [[新田原基地]]（にゅうたばるきち） - 宮崎県児湯郡新富町大字新田にある[[航空自衛隊]]の基地。 *新田原（しんでんぱら・一般的はには、しんでんはら） - 千葉県柏市東三丁目〜関場町にある柏駅東口より徒歩約17分の東武バス停留所の名称、またその周辺の地名の総称だが、新田原という地名は存在しない。一般的に増尾・沼南方面の分岐点として知られる。
== 参考文献 == * [[角川日本地名大辞典]] 各巻
== 関連項目 == * {{Intitle}} * {{Prefix}} * [[田原 (曖昧さ回避)]] * [[原田 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Aimai}} [[Category:同名の地名]]

'''新田原''' ==一覧== * 新田原（しんでんはら） - [[福島県]][[南会津郡]][[南会津町]]の地名。 * 新田原（しんでんばる） - [[福岡県]][[行橋市]]大字道場寺付近の通称。 ** [[新田原駅]]（しんでんばるえき） - 福岡県行橋市大字道場寺にある[[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[日豊本線]]の駅。近隣に[[航空自衛隊]]の[[築城基地]]があり、しばしば後述の新田原基地と混同される。 * 新田原（しんたばる） - [[長崎県]][[諫早市]][[小長井町]]の地名。小長井町新田原。旧[[北高来郡]]小長井町新田原名。 * 新田原（にゅうたばる） - [[宮崎県]][[児湯郡]][[新富町]]大字新田にある地区の通称。旧宮崎県児湯郡[[新田村 (宮崎県)|新田村]]（にゅうたそん）字新田原。 ** [[新田原基地]]（にゅうたばるきち） - 宮崎県児湯郡新富町大字新田にある[[航空自衛隊]]の基地。 *新田原（しんでんぱら・一般的はには、しんでんはら） - 千葉県柏市、県道51号市川柏線沿いにある柏駅東口より徒歩約17分の東3丁目及び大塚町にある東武バス停留所、またその周辺の地域の総称だが、新田原という地名は存在しない。一般的に柏駅より増尾・沼南方面の分岐点として知られる。

revision.text:
'''Shindehara''' (新田原)
== List ==
* Shindehara (しんでんはら) - A place name in {{ill|Minamiaizu, Fukushima|ja|南会津町}}, {{ill|Minamiaizu District, Fukushima|ja|南会津郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
* Shindenbaru (しんでんばる) - A commonly used name for an area near {{ill|Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市道場寺}}, {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. ** {{ill|Shindenbaru Station|ja|新田原駅}} (しんでんばるえき) - A station on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}} operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} (JR Kyushu), located in Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The nearby {{ill|Tsuiki Air Base|ja|築城基地}}, operated by the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}}, is often confused with the later-mentioned Nyūtabaru Air Base.
* Shintabaru (しんたばる) - A place name in {{ill|Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市小長井町}}, {{ill|Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. Specifically, {{ill|Shintabaru, Konagai Town|ja|小長井町新田原}}, formerly part of {{ill|Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki|ja|北高来郡}}.
* Nyūtabaru (にゅうたばる) - A district name in {{ill|Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町新田}}, {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|ja|児湯郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. Formerly part of {{ill|Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja|新田村 (宮崎県)}} (にゅうたそん), Nyūtabaru neighborhood. ** {{ill|Nyūtabaru Air Base|ja|新田原基地}} (にゅうたばるきち) - A {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} base located in Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki Prefecture.
* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in {{ill|Kashiwa, Chiba|ja|柏市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.
== References == * {{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} (various volumes).
== Related Topics == * {{Intitle}} * {{Prefix}} * {{ill|Tahara (disambiguation)|ja|田原 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Harada (disambiguation)|ja|原田 (曖昧さ回避)}}
{{Aimai}} [[Category:Place names with the same title]] [[ja:新田原]]

'''Shindehara''' (新田原)

* Shindenbaru (しんでんばる) - A commonly used name for an area near {{ill|Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市道場寺}}, {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. ** {{ill|Shindenbaru Station|ja|新田原駅}} (しんでんばるえき) - A station on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}} operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} (JR Kyushu), located in Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The nearby {{ill|Tsuiki Air Base|ja|築城基地}}, operated by the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}}, is often confused with the later-mentioned Nyūtabaru Air Base.

* Nyūtabaru (にゅうたばる) - A district name in {{ill|Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町新田}}, {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|ja|児湯郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. Formerly part of {{ill|Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja|新田村 (宮崎県)}} (にゅうたそん), Nyūtabaru neighborhood. ** {{ill|Nyūtabaru Air Base|ja|新田原基地}} (にゅうたばるきち) - A {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} base located in Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki Prefecture.
* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in {{ill|Kashiwa, Chiba|ja|柏市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.

It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.

'''Shindehara''' (新田原)
== List ==
* Shindehara (しんでんはら) - A place name in {{ill|Minamiaizu, Fukushima|ja|南会津町}}, {{ill|Minamiaizu District, Fukushima|ja|南会津郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
* Shindenbaru (しんでんばる) - A commonly used name for an area near {{ill|Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市道場寺}}, {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. ''' {{ill|Shindenbaru Station|ja|新田原駅}} (しんでんばるえき) - A station on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}} operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} (JR Kyushu), located in Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The nearby {{ill|Tsuiki Air Base|ja|築城基地}}, operated by the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}}, is often confused with the later-mentioned Nyūtabaru Air Base.
* Shintabaru (しんたばる) - A place name in {{ill|Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市小長井町}}, {{ill|Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. Specifically, {{ill|Shintabaru, Konagai Town|ja|小長井町新田原}}, formerly part of {{ill|Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki|ja|北高来郡}}.
* Nyūtabaru (にゅうたばる) - A district name in {{ill|Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町新田}}, {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|ja|児湯郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. Formerly part of {{ill|Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja|新田村 (宮崎県)}} (にゅうたそん), Nyūtabaru neighborhood. ''' {{ill|Nyūtabaru Air Base|ja|新田原基地}} (にゅうたばるきち) - A {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} base located in Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki Prefecture.
* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in {{ill|Kashiwa, Chiba|ja|柏市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.
== References == * {{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} (various volumes).

* Shindehara (しんでんはら) - A place name in {{ill|Minamiaizu, Fukushima|ja|南会津町}}, {{ill|Minamiaizu District, Fukushima|ja|南会津郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
* Shindenbaru (しんでんばる) - A commonly used name for an area near {{ill|Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市道場寺}}, {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. ''' {{ill|Shindenbaru Station|ja|新田原駅}} (しんでんばるえき) - A station on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}} operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} (JR Kyushu), located in Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The nearby {{ill|Tsuiki Air Base|ja|築城基地}}, operated by the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}}, is often confused with the later-mentioned Nyūtabaru Air Base.
* Shintabaru (しんたばる) - A place name in {{ill|Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市小長井町}}, {{ill|Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. Specifically, {{ill|Shintabaru, Konagai Town|ja|小長井町新田原}}, formerly part of {{ill|Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki|ja|北高来郡}}.
* Nyūtabaru (にゅうたばる) - A district name in {{ill|Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町新田}}, {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|ja|児湯郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. Formerly part of {{ill|Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja|新田村 (宮崎県)}} (にゅうたそん), Nyūtabaru neighborhood. ''' {{ill|Nyūtabaru Air Base|ja|新田原基地}} (にゅうたばるきち) - A {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} base located in Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki Prefecture.

revision.text:
'''Shindehara''' (新田原)
== List ==
* Shindehara (しんでんはら) - A place name in {{ill|Minamiaizu, Fukushima|ja|南会津町}}, {{ill|Minamiaizu District, Fukushima|ja|南会津郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
* Shindenbaru (しんでんばる) - A commonly used name for an area near {{ill|Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市道場寺}}, {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. ''' {{ill|Shindenbaru Station|ja|新田原駅}} (しんでんばるえき) - A station on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}} operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} (JR Kyushu), located in Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The nearby {{ill|Tsuiki Air Base|ja|築城基地}}, operated by the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}}, is often confused with the later-mentioned Nyūtabaru Air Base.
* Shintabaru (しんたばる) - A place name in {{ill|Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市小長井町}}, {{ill|Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. Specifically, {{ill|Shintabaru, Konagai Town|ja|小長井町新田原}}, formerly part of {{ill|Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki|ja|北高来郡}}.
* Nyūtabaru (にゅうたばる) - A district name in {{ill|Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町新田}}, {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|ja|児湯郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. Formerly part of {{ill|Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja|新田村 (宮崎県)}} (にゅうたそん), Nyūtabaru neighborhood. ''' {{ill|Nyūtabaru Air Base|ja|新田原基地}} (にゅうたばるきち) - A {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} base located in Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki Prefecture.
* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in {{ill|Kashiwa, Chiba|ja|柏市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.
== References == * {{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典}} (various volumes). == See Also! == * {{Intitle}} * {{Prefix}} * {{ill|Tahara (disambiguation)|ja|田原 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Harada (disambiguation)|ja|原田 (曖昧さ回避)}}

'''Shindehara''' (新田原)
== List ==
* Shindehara (しんでんはら) - A place name in {{ill|Minamiaizu, Fukushima|ja|南会津町}}, {{ill|Minamiaizu District, Fukushima|ja|南会津郡}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
* Shindenbaru (しんでんばる) - A commonly used name for an area near {{ill|Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市道場寺}}, {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. ''' {{ill|Shindenbaru Station|ja|新田原駅}} (しんでんばるえき) - A station on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}} operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道}} (JR Kyushu), located in Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The nearby {{ill|Tsuiki Air Base|ja|築城基地}}, operated by the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}}, is often confused with the later-mentioned Nyūtabaru Air Base.
* Shintabaru (しんたばる) - A place name in {{ill|Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市小長井町}}, {{ill|Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. Specifically, {{ill|Shintabaru, Konagai Town|ja|小長井町新田原}}, formerly part of {{ill|Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki|ja|北高来郡}}.
* Nyūtabaru (にゅうたばる) - A district name in {{ill|Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町新田}}, {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|ja|児湯郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. Formerly part of {{ill|Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja|新田村 (宮崎県)}} (にゅうたそん), Nyūtabaru neighborhood. ''' {{ill|Nyūtabaru Air Base|ja|新田原基地}} (にゅうたばるきち) - A {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} base located in Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki Prefecture.
* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in {{ill|Kashiwa, Chiba|ja|柏市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.

* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in {{ill|Kashiwa, Chiba|ja|柏市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}. It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Removing from [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:新田原 up to rev 92449392
revision.text:
'''新田原''' ==一覧== * 新田原（しんでんはら） - [[福島県]][[南会津郡]][[南会津町]]の地名。 * 新田原（しんでんばる） - [[福岡県]][[行橋市]]大字道場寺付近の通称。 ** [[新田原駅]]（しんでんばるえき） - 福岡県行橋市大字道場寺にある[[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[日豊本線]]の駅。近隣に[[航空自衛隊]]の[[築城基地]]があり、しばしば後述の新田原基地と混同される。 * 新田原（しんたばる） - [[長崎県]][[諫早市]][[小長井町]]の地名。小長井町新田原。旧[[北高来郡]]小長井町新田原名。 * 新田原（にゅうたばる） - [[宮崎県]][[児湯郡]][[新富町]]大字新田にある地区の通称。旧宮崎県児湯郡[[新田村 (宮崎県)|新田村]]（にゅうたそん）字新田原。 ** [[新田原基地]]（にゅうたばるきち） - 宮崎県児湯郡新富町大字新田にある[[航空自衛隊]]の基地。 *新田原（しんでんぱら・一般的には、しんでんはら） - 千葉県柏市、県道51号市川柏線沿いにある柏駅東口より徒歩約17分の東三丁目及び大塚町12にある東武バス停留所、またその周辺の地域の俗称だが、新田原という地名は存在しない。一般的に柏駅より増尾・沼南方面の分岐点として知られる。
== 参考文献 == * [[角川日本地名大辞典]] 各巻
== 関連項目 == * {{Intitle}} * {{Prefix}} * [[田原 (曖昧さ回避)]] * [[原田 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Aimai}} [[Category:同名の地名]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[新田原]] to [[Nyutabaru]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''新田原''' ==一覧== * 新田原（しんでんはら） - [[福島県]][[南会津郡]][[南会津町]]の地名。 * 新田原（しんでんばる） - [[福岡県]][[行橋市]]大字道場寺付近の通称。 ** [[新田原駅]]（しんでんばるえき） - 福岡県行橋市大字道場寺にある[[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[日豊本線]]の駅。近隣に[[航空自衛隊]]の[[築城基地]]があり、しばしば後述の新田原基地と混同される。 * 新田原（しんたばる） - [[長崎県]][[諫早市]][[小長井町]]の地名。小長井町新田原。旧[[北高来郡]]小長井町新田原名。 * 新田原（にゅうたばる） - [[宮崎県]][[児湯郡]][[新富町]]大字新田にある地区の通称。旧宮崎県児湯郡[[新田村 (宮崎県)|新田村]]（にゅうたそん）字新田原。 ** [[新田原基地]]（にゅうたばるきち） - 宮崎県児湯郡新富町大字新田にある[[航空自衛隊]]の基地。 *新田原（しんでんぱら・一般的には、しんでんはら） - 千葉県柏市、県道51号市川柏線沿いにある柏駅東口より徒歩約17分の東三丁目及び大塚町12にある東武バス停留所、またその周辺の地域の俗称だが、新田原という地名は存在しない。一般的に柏駅より増尾・沼南方面の分岐点として知られる。
== 参考文献 == * [[角川日本地名大辞典]] 各巻
== 関連項目 == * {{Intitle}} * {{Prefix}} * [[田原 (曖昧さ回避)]] * [[原田 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{Aimai}} [[Category:同名の地名]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shindehara''' (新田原)

== List ==

* Shindehara (しんでんはら) - A place name in [[:en:Minamiaizu|Minamiaizu, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamiaizu District, Fukushima|Minamiaizu District, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].

* Shindenbaru (しんでんばる) - A commonly used name for an area near {{ill|Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市道場寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]].
** [[:en:Shindembaru Station|Shindenbaru Station]] (しんでんばるえき) - A station on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] (JR Kyushu), located in Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The nearby [[:en:Tsuiki Air Field|Tsuiki Air Base]], operated by the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]], is often confused with the later-mentioned Nyūtabaru Air Base.

* Shintabaru (しんたばる) - A place name in {{ill|Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市小長井町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Isahaya, Nagasaki|Isahaya, Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Specifically, {{ill|Shintabaru, Konagai Town|ja|小長井町新田原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintabaru, Konagai Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, formerly part of [[:en:Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki|Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki]].

* Nyūtabaru (にゅうたばる) - A district name in {{ill|Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町新田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Koyu District, Miyazaki|Koyu District, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]]. Formerly part of {{ill|Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja|新田村 (宮崎県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (にゅうたそん), Nyūtabaru neighborhood.
** [[:en:Nyutabaru Air Base|Nyūtabaru Air Base]] (にゅうたばるきち) - A [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] base located in Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki Prefecture.

* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.

== References ==
* {{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary|qq=|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4093011}} (various volumes).
== See Also ==
* {{Intitle}}
* {{Prefix}}
* {{ill|Tahara (disambiguation)|ja|田原 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahara (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田原|1=Tahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q419035}}
* {{ill|Harada (disambiguation)|ja|原田 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada (disambiguation)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 原田|1=Harada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q352910}}

* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. * Shindehara (しんでんはら) - A place name in {{ill|Minamiaizu|en|Minamiaizu|WD=Q1205058|lt=Minamiaizu, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Minamiaizu District, Fukushima|en|Minamiaizu District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155802|lt=Minamiaizu District, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
* Shindenbaru (しんでんばる) - A commonly used name for an area near {{ill|Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja|行橋市道場寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dojoji, Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|en|Yukuhashi, Fukuoka|WD=Q851668|lt=Yukuhashi, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. ** {{ill|Shindembaru Station|en|Shindembaru Station|WD=Q5360079|lt=Shindenbaru Station}} (しんでんばるえき) - A station on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} (JR Kyushu), located in Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture. The nearby {{ill|Tsuiki Air Field|en|Tsuiki Air Field|WD=Q255228|lt=Tsuiki Air Base}}, operated by the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|WD=Q830305|lt=Japan Air Self-Defense Force}}, is often confused with the later-mentioned Nyūtabaru Air Base.
* Shintabaru (しんたばる) - A place name in {{ill|Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja|諫早市小長井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konagai Town, Isahaya, Nagasaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Isahaya, Nagasaki|en|Isahaya, Nagasaki|WD=Q596469|lt=Isahaya, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. Specifically, {{ill|Shintabaru, Konagai Town|ja|小長井町新田原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintabaru, Konagai Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, formerly part of {{ill|Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki|en|Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki|WD=Q6417903|lt=Kitatakaki District, Nagasaki}}.
* Nyūtabaru (にゅうたばる) - A district name in {{ill|Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja|新富町新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|en|Shintomi, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206753|lt=Shintomi, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en|Koyu District, Miyazaki|WD=Q1132169|lt=Koyu District, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}. Formerly part of {{ill|Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja|新田村 (宮崎県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyūta Village, Miyazaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (にゅうたそん), Nyūtabaru neighborhood. ** {{ill|Nyutabaru Air Base|en|Nyutabaru Air Base|WD=Q3015913|lt=Nyūtabaru Air Base}} (にゅうたばるきち) - A {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|WD=Q830305|lt=Japan Air Self-Defense Force}} base located in Nyūta, Shintomi, Miyazaki Prefecture.
* Shindenpara (しんでんぱら, generally read as しんでんはら) - An unofficial name used to describe the area near Tobu Bus stops located in Higashi 3-chome and Otsuka-cho 12, along Prefectural Route 51 (Ichikawa-Kashiwa Line) in {{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is roughly a 17-minute walk from the east exit of Kashiwa Station. Although there is no official place name "Shindenpara," the area is commonly known as a branching point from Kashiwa Station toward Masuo and Shonan.
